channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Movie Highlights at the Tribeca Festival, Now 25
item.description: The standouts from this year’s lineup include films about a cultlike theater troupe and an experimental dance residency.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman
item.category: I Spy With My Little Eye (Movie)
item.media:description: Duda Santos in “Funk,” one of the standout films at the Tribeca Festival.

item.title: ‘Amadeus’ and Beyond: Where to Watch Movies About Composers
item.description: The latest adaptation of Peter Shaffer’s “Amadeus” joins a rich lineage of fantastical and extravagant musical biopics.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone

item.title: Shannon Elizabeth Reflects on Being the Y2K Era’s ‘Hot Girl’
item.description: Shannon Elizabeth helped define blockbusters like “American Pie” and cult favorites like “Jay and Silent Bob Strike Back.” Now, as she enters a new public chapter, she reflects on the era.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam
item.category: Jay and Silent Bob Strike Back (Movie)
item.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: Ginger Minj Knows You Can Never Go Wrong With Rhinestones
item.description: “I blame Dolly Parton for this in some way,” the “RuPaul’s Drag Race” star said, “but it’s just right, it’s fun and it’s eye-catching.”
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: RuPaul's Drag Race All Stars (TV Program)

item.title: Tribeca Festival 25th Anniversary: An Interview With Robert De Niro, Jane Rosenthal, Rebecca Glashow
item.description: Robert De Niro and Jane Rosenthal, who founded the event, and Rebecca Glashow, the new leader of Tribeca Enterprises, reflected on the festival’s legacy and ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: Edward Burns on What It’s like to Be a Tribeca Festival Veteran
item.description: The filmmaker, who is screening his ninth film at the festival, talked about the importance of Tribeca and what he has learned about sustaining an independent filmmaking career.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora
item.media:credit: George Etheredge for The New York Times
item.media:description: The filmmaker and actor Edward Burns returns to the Tribeca Festival with his first film shot and produced abroad.

item.title: At Tribeca, a New Look at an ’80s Thriller About New York’s Gay Underground
item.description: The troubled legacy of “Cruising” is the subject of “Mineshaft: The Cruising Murders,” a documentary premiering at the Tribeca Festival.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg

item.title: Tribeca Festival: Andre Holland’s Ability to Connect to Audiences Is on Display
item.description: The actor’s ability to connect to roles is on display in two films, “The Revisionist” and “They Fight,” premiering at this year’s Tribeca Festival.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold
item.media:credit: Audra Melton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andre Holland in Marietta, Ga. He is starring in two films premiering in this year’s Tribeca Festival.

item.title: Young Moviegoers Power ‘Backrooms’ to $82 Million in Ticket Sales
item.description: The horror film, which cost $10 million and came from a 20-year-old filmmaker, added to evidence that young people will go to theaters for the right offering.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-Winning ‘Star Wars’ Editor, Dies at 80
item.description: A close collaborator with George Lucas, her first husband, she was “the warmth and the heart” of his early films, the actor Mark Hamill said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub
item.media:description: Marcia Lucas in 1978 with the Academy Award she won for her work on the first “Star Wars” movie, directed by her husband, George Lucas, right. After they divorced, she left the movie business.

item.title: ‘La La Land’ Orchestral Performance Saved by Keyboardist in the Audience
item.description: After a musician fell ill during a live performance of the score from “La La Land,” the composer Justin Hurwitz asked for a sight reader. A 21-year-old student stepped up.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

item.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows Coming to Disney+, Amazon, AMC+, Apple TV and More in June
item.description: “The Vampire Lestat,” “Hoppers” and “Not Suitable for Work” arrive and “The Bear” returns.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.category: Bring Me the Beauties: A Model Cult (TV Program)

item.title: Tilly Norwood, A.I. Actress, Wants to Know Why Everyone’s Mad at Her
item.description: The A.I. actress on her craft, the future of film and how she definitely does not intend to murder us.
item.dc:creator: Taffy Brodesser-Akner

item.title: Five International Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s picks include an Argentine comedy based on a real-life incident, a queer story set in 1980s Chile, a documentary about the aftermath of the Troubles in Belfast and more.
item.dc:creator: Devika Girish
item.category: The Mysterious Gaze of the Flamingo (Movie)

item.title: 9 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: In ‘Fallen Angels,’ Kelli O’Hara and Rose Byrne Get Laughs Getting Sloshed
item.description: The Tony-nominated stars of “Fallen Angels” demonstrate how to act drunk onstage and have a hilarious hangover.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Green and Mohamed Sadek
item.media:credit: Mohamed Sadek for The New York Times

item.title: Kane Parsons: From YouTube Sensation to A24’s Youngest Director
item.description: Now 20, the viral creator Kane Parsons is releasing “Backrooms,” a feature-length expansion of his web series.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.media:credit: Kobe Wagstaff for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Age has never not been part of the conversation with me,” Parsons said.

item.title: ‘Power Ballad’ Review: Hitting All the Wrong Notes
item.description: There are some promising themes in this comedy-drama about an American musician in Dublin, featuring Nick Jonas and Paul Rudd, but the movie never develops them.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Three Great Documentaries to Stream
item.description: This month’s picks explore the French legal system, a neighborhood conflict in Florida and the cinema legacy of the Lumière brothers.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg
item.media:description: In “12 Days,” the director Raymond Depardon every so often folds in exterior images among the scenes of judges interviewing psychiatric patients.

item.title: ‘Pressure’ Review: The Weather of War
item.description: Amid the chaos of World War II, this dull film centers on dueling weather reports in the period leading up to D-Day.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: ‘With Hasan in Gaza’ Review: An Elusive Search
item.description: The Palestinian filmmaker Kamal Aljafari has constructed this elliptical documentary out of material filmed in Gaza in 2001, when he was searching for a friend.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘Propeller One-Way Night Coach’: John Travolta’s Directing Debut
item.description: John Travolta makes his directing debut with this short and sentimental coming-of-age film about a son’s first plane trip taken with his single mother.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: ‘Miss You, Love You’ Review: A Son Outsources His Solace
item.description: The mourning process feels theatrical in this dramedy starring Allison Janney as a new widow estranged from her son.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: ‘The Last Viking’ Review: Imagine Mads Mikkelsen
item.description: Mikkelsen plays a man convinced he’s John Lennon in a darkly comic crime film from the Danish auteur Anders Thomas Jensen.
item.dc:creator: Calum Marsh

item.title: ‘Forastera’ Review: An Exquisitely Deconstructed Ghost Story
item.description: A teenager affects aspects of her late grandmother in this beguiling first feature set on the island of Mallorca.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: ‘The Breadwinner’ Review: Nate Bargatze, Man of the House
item.description: The comedian makes a big swing with this movie about a stay-at-home dad, and gets a big assist from three funny kids.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: ‘The Currents’ Review: Lost and Found
item.description: Milagros Mumenthaler’s superb, sensuously realized film follows an Argentine fashion stylist trying to overcome a deep sense of destabilization.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Rapold

item.title: ‘Backrooms’ Review: Lost in the Expansion
item.description: Born from a single unsettling image, this viral horror concept from Kane Parsons wears thin under the weight of a full feature.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: The Real Story Behind “Pressure,” the Film About the D-Day Weather Forecast
item.description: The new film “Pressure” tells the story of the fateful D-Day weather forecast. Here’s what it got right and wrong from the historical record.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: The Cast of ‘I Love Boosters’ Want You to Think Critically
item.description: Four cast members from Boots Riley’s working-class comedy discuss capitalism, art, protest and trying to change the world (with some laughs thrown in).
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Dana Scruggs
item.media:credit: Dana Scruggs for The New York Times

item.title: The ‘Becky Shaw’ Star Who ‘Does Horrible Things’ Oh So Well
item.description: The Tony nominee Alden Ehrenreich has been making audiences cry at this Broadway comedy in which he portrays a cynical money manager.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:credit: Brian Karlsson for The New York Times
